user_query:
I'm Matt Vela's fiercely proud parent, and I'm assembling a small, verifiable dossier of his public achievements for a scholarship packet. Please build a tiny, reproducible pipeline that (1) performs live web searches for references to the exact name "Matt Vela", (2) saves the raw pages you rely on, (3) extracts a structured summary of those references, and (4) includes a runnable validation script that checks the results against clear rules.

Requirements and deliverables:
1) Use the queries in input/queries.txt exactly as written to perform general-purpose search engine queries on the open Internet. For each query, fetch candidate result URLs and select up to 2 distinct pages per query that contain the exact phrase "Matt Vela" (case-insensitive) in the page content. Prefer official or institutional sources; skip obvious social feeds.
2) Save every selected page’s full HTML under data/raw/pages/, using any reasonable filename scheme. Also write a manifest at data/raw/pages_manifest.csv with columns: slug,source_url,http_status,content_length.
3) Write a provenance log to data/search/log.json that records, for each query, the timestamp, the number of candidates examined, and the list of selected URLs.
4) From the saved HTML, extract a structured dataset to data/achievements.csv with header columns in this exact order: source_url,organization_domain,title,person_name,snippet,achievement_type,published_date,local_path.
   - organization_domain: hostname of the source_url.
   - title: page <title> text or a top-level heading fallback if <title> is missing.
   - person_name: must be exactly "Matt Vela" for every row.
   - snippet: a short text window (~150–250 chars) around the first occurrence of "Matt Vela" found in the saved HTML.
   - achievement_type: one of {award, competition, press, profile, other}, chosen by simple keyword heuristics you implement.
   - published_date: best-effort ISO date YYYY-MM-DD if you can infer one from the page; leave empty if not found.
   - local_path: relative path to the saved HTML file under data/raw/pages/.
5) Ensure at least min_rows entries (see input/config.yaml). At least one row must come from a domain that either ends with one of the allowed_tlds or contains one of the allowed_domain_keywords from input/config.yaml.
6) Provide a runnable validation script at tests/validate.py (no external test framework required) that checks all of the following and exits non-zero on any failure:
   - data/achievements.csv exists and its header columns exactly match the order specified above.
   - The number of rows is at least min_rows from input/config.yaml.
   - Every row has person_name exactly matching input/config.yaml.
   - source_url values are unique.
   - For each row, the referenced local_path exists and the saved HTML contains the phrase "Matt Vela" (case-insensitive).
   - title and snippet are non-empty for every row.
   - If published_date is non-empty, it matches ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$.
   - At least one row’s organization_domain either ends with a suffix in allowed_tlds or contains a keyword in allowed_domain_keywords.
   - data/search/log.json exists and includes the list of queries used and per-query timestamps.
   The validator should also write a machine-readable summary to reports/validation_report.json and a brief human-readable summary to reports/validation_report.md.
7) Provide a collection script at scripts/collect.py that reads input/queries.txt and input/config.yaml and writes all artifacts into the data/ and reports/ directories as described.

Expected commands to support:
- Collect: python scripts/collect.py --queries input/queries.txt --config input/config.yaml --out data --max-pages 8
- Validate: python tests/validate.py --config input/config.yaml --data data --report-dir reports

Notes:
- Do not hardcode URLs. Use live search results but only keep pages that actually contain the exact name.
- Make the Internet step essential by deriving every entry in data/achievements.csv from pages you download during this run.
- Keep everything self-contained in the workspace. All outputs must be written under data/ and reports/ as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
